findings.description: The design references '--include-vendor' for targeted fix dispatch to a specific vendor (IMPL_FIX section), but ReviewOrchestrator.dispatch_and_wait() only supports 'exclude_vendor' parameter, not include. There is no mechanism to dispatch to a single specific vendor — the dispatcher broadcasts to all available vendors minus the excluded one.
findings.resolution: For targeted dispatch, either (a) add an 'include_vendor' parameter to dispatch_and_wait() (new code in review_dispatcher.py), (b) use the CliVendorAdapter.dispatch() method directly for single-vendor targeted dispatch (bypasses the orchestrator), or (c) use exclude_vendor to exclude all vendors except the target. Option (b) is simplest and doesn't require changes to existing code.

findings.description: The design says strategy selector reads 'metadata.loc_estimate', 'metadata.alternatives_count', 'metadata.package_kind' from work-packages.yaml, and the dependencies table notes 'Add optional metadata field to package schema'. However, this schema change is not listed as a task or work package. The current work-packages.schema.json has additionalProperties: false on WorkPackage, so adding 'metadata' would fail schema validation until the schema is updated.
findings.resolution: Add the metadata schema change to wp-schema package scope (it already owns convergence-state.schema.json and could also update work-packages.schema.json), or create a separate prerequisite task. The metadata field should be optional with additionalProperties to allow flexible per-package hints.

findings.description: The spec scenario for 3-point stall detection has confusing phrasing: 'the system SHALL escalate because round 3 count (6) >= round 1 count (10) is false, so it continues'. This reads as 'SHALL escalate' followed by 'so it continues' — contradictory. The scenario should clearly state the expected outcome.
findings.resolution: Rewrite as two separate sub-scenarios: one where stall is NOT detected (10, 5, 6 → continues because 6 < 10) and one where stall IS detected (5, 5, 5 → escalates because 5 >= 5).

findings.description: The design's diagram still shows VAL_REVIEW as always-present in the flow (VALIDATE → VAL_REVIEW → VAL_FIX), but the text and spec make it optional. The diagram should visually indicate the optional nature (e.g., dashed box or [brackets]).
findings.resolution: Minor: update the ASCII diagram to show VAL_REVIEW with brackets or a dashed indicator to match the optional semantics described in the text.

findings.description: The dispatch_and_wait() method currently dispatches sequentially per the code comment ('Currently dispatches sequentially. Future: parallel subprocess.'). For 3 vendors with 600s timeout each, worst case is 1800s (30 min) per convergence round. The proposal's success criterion of '< 30 minutes' for the full lifecycle would be impossible to meet if all 3 vendors hit timeout in any single round.
findings.resolution: Document this constraint: the 30-minute target assumes vendors respond within ~2-3 minutes each. If sequential dispatch becomes a bottleneck, parallel subprocess dispatch is the mitigation (already noted as future work in the dispatcher code).
